messages.content: Finishing up the auth milestone. Everything worked but it took 3 weeks instead of 1.5 because I had to debug JWT expiry edge cases and deal with an OAuth library that had breaking changes.
What did you actually deliver in 'Auth & Onboarding'? Walk me through it — what's working and testable right now?

Magic link email works — users get a link, click it, land logged in with a JWT session. Google OAuth runs as a fallback. JWT session management handles expiry and refresh. The onboarding flow walks new users through their first JD prompt. It's all connected — from zero to generating a JD in one flow.
What created the most friction on this milestone? Be specific — was it the task, the estimate, a dependency, or something about how you worked?

Two things compounded. JWT expiry had subtle clock-skew and refresh-race edge cases I didn't anticipate — nearly 2 days of debugging. Then passport-google-oauth20 published a breaking change in a minor version bump the exact week I was working on it. Had to dig through their changelog and rewrite the callback handling. Neither was in the estimate at all.
One thing you'd do differently on the next milestone?

Pin dependency versions at the start of the milestone, not only when something breaks. And build a +20% buffer into any estimate that depends on a library I haven't touched in the last 6 months. For JWT specifically I'd write a test matrix for expiry scenarios before calling it done — I only found the edge cases in manual testing.

project.title: HR Copilot
project.one_liner: AI tool for HR teams to automate JD writing and screening

success_criteria.criterion: Beta live for design partners within 6 weeks
